mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer and capital demand, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods, or 60-day forward returns show no significant difference from baseline when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflationary pressure, prompting market participants to price in tighter monetary policy and generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during periods of stable wage growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and expanding household income, which supports aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative mean during payroll expansion periods, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarter-over-quarter expansion in real GDP and month-over-month growth in retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength, elevating corporate earnings forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when inventory accumulation exceeds sales growth, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month acceleration in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals supply-side bottlenecks and impending inflationary pressures, prompting defensive portfolio positioning and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0% in late-cycle periods, or the signal predictive power vanishes when capacity utilization remains below 78%.
